prompt: Build the epic designer: flow's epic-layer design phase that turns an epic prompt into a reviewed design.md + a dependency DAG of PR-sized features (manifest.json), self-validated against the schema and DAG checkers, then stops.

features.title: Epic manifest schema + path contract + validator
features.description: Add bin/lib/epic-manifest-schema.ts (EpicManifest/Feature types + isEpicManifest type guard, mirroring state.ts), a bare-name CLI validator wired into discoverValidators (mirroring the four existing *-schema.ts validators), the .flow/epics/<slug>/{design.md,manifest.json} path contract, and bin/lib/epic-manifest-schema.test.ts. Runnable as one flow new PR.
features.rationale: Hides D1 (manifest shape + storage path) — the most-depended-on contract; the walking-skeleton root every other feature consumes.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN given a well-formed manifest THE SYSTEM SHALL exit 0 and print {"ok":true}
WHEN given a malformed manifest THE SYSTEM SHALL exit non-zero with a reason on stderr

features.title: DAG validation helper (acyclic / orphan / ready-set)
features.description: Add bin/flow-epic-dag.ts — pure Kahn's-algorithm cycle detection, orphan-edge + unique-id + self-dependency checks, and a ready-set frontier; --validate <path> exits non-zero naming the offending cycle/edge. Tests cover empty, linear chain, diamond, disconnected components, cycle, and orphan-edge cases. Runnable as one flow new PR.
features.rationale: Hides D2 (the graph-correctness algorithm); makes a decomposition mechanically trustworthy. Consumes F1's Feature[]/dependsOn shape.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN given an acyclic orphan-free manifest THE SYSTEM SHALL exit 0
WHEN given a cycle THE SYSTEM SHALL exit non-zero and name the cycle

features.title: flow epic CLI verb + epic-id + epic state
features.description: Add 'epic' to bin/lib/verbs.ts; bin/lib/epic.ts (runEpicCli dispatching design/run/status/ls, with run/status/ls as loud deferred stubs); mint an epic-id via slug.ts; minimal epic-state read/write; a runVerb case in bin/flow; completion entries; tests. Independently mergeable verb skeleton. Runnable as one flow new PR.
features.rationale: Hides D3 (CLI invocation surface + epic identity/state storage). Consumes F1's manifest/path contract it reads and writes.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN flow epic --help runs THE SYSTEM SHALL print usage and exit 0
WHEN flow epic create --help runs THE SYSTEM SHALL short-circuit before any side effect

features.title: Epic-grain designer logic → design.md + manifest.json
features.description: Add the one-shot epic-grain /product-planning discovery methodology (sibling references/epic-discovery-instructions.md, selected by a MODE: epic spawn flag) implementing 02 §4 pipeline ①: EARS acceptance criteria, ADR-shaped design decisions = the Parnas volatile-decision list, Parnas/Simon vertical-slice decomposition, a Mermaid DAG, and Open Questions; emit a six-section design.md + a schema-valid manifest.json to the F1 path, self-validating via both checkers in a fix-and-re-run loop. Runnable as one flow new PR.
features.rationale: Hides D4 (the design methodology) — the most volatile part, isolated behind the stable manifest contract; where MVP value lands. Consumes F1 (emits a schema-valid manifest) and F2 (self-checks its own DAG).
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN run on an epic prompt THE SYSTEM SHALL write a six-section design.md and a manifest.json that passes both validators (exit 0)
WHEN the decomposition is inspected THE SYSTEM SHALL contain only vertical-slice features with produced/consumed edges

features.title: flow epic create end-to-end + approval checkpoint
features.description: Wire flow epic create '<prompt>' → mint id (F3) → fire the materiality-gated clarification round (AskUserQuestion, the caller's own authorized form) → spawn the one-shot designer (F4) on the clarified prompt → write artifacts → run F1+F2 validators as a gate → surface design.md at an epic-design-pending-review checkpoint (approve/redirect/cancel); stop with no execution. Runnable as one flow new PR.
features.rationale: Hides D5 (the end-to-end flow + human approval contract); the diamond-closing integration. Consumes F3's verb entry and F4's designer.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN flow epic create '<sample epic>' runs THE SYSTEM SHALL write committed artifacts, pass both validators, and halt at the checkpoint without launching or merging
WHEN the developer redirects at the checkpoint THE SYSTEM SHALL re-run the designer with the redirect appended
